content.problemStatement:
As usual, I went to work in the morning. Unfortunately, I found out that my manager bought a new machine and I have to learn to operate it.

There are NNN boxes in a line (numbered 111 through NNN). Initially, the boxes are empty, and I need to use the machine to put tokens in them. For each valid iii, the iii-th box has a maximum capacity SiS_iSi​ tokens. I can perform the following operation any number of times: choose an integer LLL (1≤L≤N1 \le L \le N1≤L≤N) and put one token in each of the boxes 1,2,…,L1, 2, \ldots, L1,2,…,L.

My manager told me to put as many tokens as possible into the boxes in total (the distribution of tokens in the boxes does not matter). Of course, it is not allowed to perform an operation that would result in the number of tokens in some box exceeding its capacity. Can you help me calculate the maximum number of tokens that can be put in these boxes?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1065 \cdot 10^65⋅106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the maximum number of tokens.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The optimal way is to perform the following operations:

Choose L=3L = 3L=3 and use the machine to put one token in each box.
Choose L=1L = 1L=1 and use the machine to put one more token in the first box.
